'''John Wilshere''' (born 5 May 1978) is a [[Papua New Guinea]]n former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played for the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]].<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Playing career==
Wilshere made his first grade rugby league debut with the [[Western Reds]] during the [[1997 Super League (Australia) season|1997 Super League season]]. He scored one try from four appearances with the Reds, before moving to the [[Melbourne Storm]] for the 1998 season. He would later spent time at the [[Penrith Panthers]] before joining the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]] in 2003, where he made 10 NRL appearances.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Elbra |first1=Tim |title=Death, drugs and dirty deeds: A brief, bizarre history of every Western Reds player |url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2025-westernperth-reds-players-every-story-as-bears-brings-rugby-league-back-to-western-australia/news-story/08a33eddab3610177b83db46342ae19b |website=foxsports.com.au |publisher=News Corporation Australia |access-date=17 September 2025 |date=1 May 2025}}</ref>

Wilshere had represented Papua New Guinea many times and was a key player in their squad at the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]] in Australia.

Wilshere scored 538 points for the [[Norths Devils]] in the [[Queensland Cup]].<ref name="NORTHS DEVILS">{{cite web|url=http://www.qrl.com.au/default.aspx?s=qldcup-clubs-norths |title=NORTHS DEVILS |publisher=QRL |year=2010 |access-date=2010-03-15 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110218231418/http://www.qrl.com.au/default.aspx?s=qldcup-clubs-norths |archive-date=18 February 2011 }}</ref>

Wilshere scored 358 points for the [[Eastern Suburbs Tigers|Easts Tigers]] in the [[Queensland Cup]].<ref name="Easts Tigers">{{cite web|url=http://www.qrl.com.au/default.aspx?s=qldcup-clubs-easts|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110218231015/http://www.qrl.com.au/default.aspx?s=qldcup-clubs-easts|url-status=dead|archive-date=2011-02-18|title=Easts Tigers|publisher=QRL|year=2010|access-date=2010-03-15}}</ref>

Continuing his career in England, Wilshere played with the [[Warrington Wolves]], [[Leigh Centurions]], and [[Salford City Reds]] in the [[Super League]] competition.

==Representative career==
Wilshere was captain of the [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="PNG name preliminary squad">{{cite web|url=http://www.rlwc08.com/news/news/archive/2008/08/03/png-name-preliminary-squad.aspx |title=PNG name preliminary squad |publisher=RLWC.com |date=2008-08-01 |access-date=2008-08-06 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100410150913/http://www.rlwc08.com/news/news/archive/2008/08/03/png-name-preliminary-squad.aspx |archive-date=10 April 2010 }}</ref> Wilshere was named as part of the [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] squad for the [[2009 Pacific Cup]].<ref name="Kumuls named">{{cite web|url=http://www.thenational.com.pg/?q=node/1554|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120226210618/http://www.thenational.com.pg/?q=node%2F1554|url-status=dead|archive-date=26 February 2012|title=Kumuls named|publisher=thenational.com.pg|date=12 October 2009|access-date=1 January 2010}}</ref>

On 7 November 2009, Wilshere announced his retirement from the Kumuls.<ref name="Wilshere 2680">{{cite web|url=http://www.thenational.com.pg/?q=node/2680|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120226210635/http://www.thenational.com.pg/?q=node%2F2680|url-status=dead|archive-date=26 February 2012|title=Statistics a Wilshere 2680|publisher=thenational.com.pg|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018}}</ref>
